subject: [RFC v2 06/21] mm: thp: handle split failure in wp_huge_pmd()

2026-02-26.patch_summary: wp_huge_pmd() splits the PMD when COW or write-notify must be handled at PTE level (e.g., shared/file VMAs, userfaultfd).  It then returns VM_FAULT_FALLBACK so the fault handler retries at PTE granularity. If the split fails, the PMD is still huge.  The PTE fault path cannot handle a huge PMD entry. Return VM_FAULT_OOM on split failure, which signals the fault handler to invoke the OOM killer or return -ENOMEM to userspace. This is similar to what __handle_mm_fault would do if p4d_alloc or pud_alloc fails.
